flashcards.front: What is a Relation in the Relational Model?
flashcards.back: A Relation is a two-dimensional table with rows (tuples) and columns (attributes). It has a unique name and contains atomic (indivisible) values.
A Primary Key is a minimal set of attributes that uniquely identifies each tuple in a relation. It cannot contain NULL values and must have unique values.
A Foreign Key is an attribute or set of attributes that references the Primary Key of another relation. It establishes a relationship between two tables.
A Candidate Key is a set of attributes that can uniquely identify tuples. A relation can have multiple candidate keys; one is chosen as the Primary Key.
A Super Key is a set of one or more attributes that uniquely identifies tuples. It may contain extra attributes beyond what's minimally needed.
A Composite Key is a Primary Key consisting of two or more attributes. Neither attribute alone can uniquely identify tuples.
Entity Integrity states that the Primary Key cannot have NULL values. Every tuple must have a unique, non-null identifier.
Referential Integrity requires that foreign key values in a referencing table must either match a Primary Key value in the referenced table or be NULL.
Domain Constraint specifies that each attribute must have a value from its defined domain (set of permissible values). Values must be atomic.
A Tuple is a single row in a relation. It represents a collection of related data values (attributes) for a specific entity.
An Attribute is a named column in a relation representing a characteristic of the entity. Each attribute has a domain.

flashcards.front: Define Degree and Cardinality of a relation.
flashcards.back: Degree is the number of attributes (columns) in a relation. Cardinality is the number of tuples (rows) in a relation.

flashcards.front: What is a Null Value in databases?
flashcards.back: Null represents missing or unknown information. It is not zero or blank; it indicates the absence of a value.
An Alternate Key is any Candidate Key that is not selected as the Primary Key. It can uniquely identify rows but is not the primary identifier.
A Weak Entity is an entity that cannot be uniquely identified by its own attributes and requires a Foreign Key from a strong entity to identify it.
